content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with atom C:17 highlighted. Looking at the image, I can clearly identify the neighbor atoms connected to C:17:

- N:16 (nitrogen, index 16) - bonded to C:17
- C:18 (carbon, index 18) - bonded to C:17  
- H:31 (hydrogen, index 31) - bonded to C:17

From the molecular structure shown, C:17 appears to be part of a chain where it's connected to nitrogen (N:16), another carbon (C:18), and a hydrogen atom (H:31). These are all covalently bonded to C:17.

Regarding the geomconf_index, based on the file name "geomconf_3898.xyz", the geomconf_index for this molecule is 3898.

So the neighbor atoms of C:17 are:
- Atom index 16, element N
- Atom index 18, element C  
- Atom index 31, element H

Geomconf_index: 3898

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 17, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
